content: Just as the language learning definition of reflexion frames it as both a passive reflection of incoming input and an active metacognitive practice to refine one’s own output, existing LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be reworked to integrate this dual function, rather than only focusing on active post-hoc error correction. This dual framing of reflexion suggests current LLM self-reflection underuses the passive 'mirroring' step of surfacing inconsistencies between the model’s implicit understanding and observed task feedback.
